compiled_state.title: Write UPDATE test for session-safety-check.sh dual-read behavior (new + legacy log path)
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## testing_mode: UPDATE

## What
Add a RED test to tests/hooks/test-session-safety.sh that verifies session-safety-check.sh reads error counts from BOTH the new log path (~/.claude/logs/dso-hook-errors.jsonl) AND the legacy path (~/.claude/hook-error-log.jsonl) during the migration window.

## File Impact
| Action | File |
|--------|------|
| MODIFY | tests/hooks/test-session-safety.sh |

## Test to Add
test_session_safety_reads_legacy_path():
  - Write a recent error entry to legacy path only (TEST_HOME/.claude/hook-error-log.jsonl)
  - Write a recent error entry to new path only (TEST_HOME/.claude/logs/dso-hook-errors.jsonl)
  - Run session-safety-check.sh
  - Assert: both entries contribute to the hook error count (not just one path)

This test must FAIL (RED) before T4 migrates session-safety-check.sh to dual-read.

## Depends On
- 4fd9-7d9c (T2 — hook-error-handler.sh must exist so session-safety-check.sh can source it)
